---
title: "subtitle_block"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L479)

# `subtitle_block()`

Create a Subtitle Block Plot

## Usage

```r
subtitle_block(
  subtitle,
  subtitle_size = 10,
  halign = "left",
  lineheight = 1.2,
  margin_top = 0,
  margin_bottom = 5,
  margin_left = 5,
  margin_right = 5,
  width = 0.95,
  wrap_width = NULL,
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `subtitle` | Subtitle text (marquee-formatted). Supports markdown formatting: **bold**, *italic*, {#color text. |
| `subtitle_size` | Size for subtitle in pts. Default: 10. |
| `halign` | Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", or "right". Default: "left". |
| `lineheight` | Line height multiplier. Default: 1.2. |
| `margin_top` | Top margin in pts. Default: 0. |
| `margin_bottom` | Bottom margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_left` | Left margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_right` | Right margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `width` | Maximum width for text wrapping (0-1). Default: 0.95. |
| `wrap_width` | Character width to wrap text at. Default: NULL (no wrapping). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |

## Value

A ggplot object that can be combined with other plots via patchwork.

## Description

Creates a standalone subtitle element as a ggplot for use in patchwork
compositions.

## Details

Subtitles provide context and supporting information below the main title.
Use for timeframes, data descriptions, or methodological notes. Supports
marquee markdown: **bold**, *italic*, and {#hexcolor text.

Place below `title_block()` using patchwork's `/` operator.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(patchwork)

subtitle_plot <- subtitle_block(
    "Fruit category performance, October-December 2024"
)

title_plot / subtitle_plot / my_chart +
    plot_layout(heights = c(0.08, 0.05, 0.87))

## End(Not run)
```
